_description: Hardened causal-modularity verdict for Command A+ — the decisive run. Per-family expert ablation vs a size-16 random-expert null, scored as per-token NLL on an INDEPENDENT corpus (Wikipedia, distinct from the routing-probe/Belebele corpus); capabilities via teacher-forced solution NLL. Bootstrap 95% CIs. This is Table 1 of the paper.
ablation: router-mask + gate renormalization (16/128 experts), inference-time only

decision_rules.point: on-target effect > random null (mean+2sigma) AND worst within-domain off-target <= on-target/3
decision_rules.conservative_CI: lower 95% CI of on-target > null(mean+2sigma) AND upper 95% CI of worst off-target <= (lower 95% CI of on-target)/3

metric: per-item NLL increase (nats/token) on the family's own axis; off-target = within-domain other axes

ar.note: robust module across corpus, metric, and CIs

es.note: corpus-dependent: bleeds into Arabic on Wikipedia (es/ar overlap in only 2 of 16 experts {30,86}, Jaccard 0.067; mechanism not isolated); knife-edge (passes point rule by 0.002)

math.note: metric-dependent: entangled with general reasoning under task accuracy; selectivity does not survive CIs

code.note: real effect but entangled (bleeds into math); fails point rule by 0.009

general.note: does not beat its own null

summary.modular_point_rule: 3/6 (ar, es, math); count threshold-sensitive: es passes selectivity by 0.002, code (rejected) fails by 0.009; ar and math clear comfortably
summary.headline: Robust functional modularity is rare and measurement-dependent; only the Arabic family survives across corpus, metric, and a conservative statistical bar.
